records.title: Cart subtotal sums each line's list price
records.intent: A storefront quote starts from the cart subtotal: the sum of unit list price times quantity across every line. With no discounts or tax configured, the order total equals the subtotal.
records.given: A cart has one or more lines, each added with a SKU, unit price, and quantity.
records.when: getQuote is called with no discounts, coupons, or tax configured.
records.then: subtotal equals the sum of unitPrice times quantity for every line, each line's lineTotal equals its unitPrice times quantity, and total equals subtotal.

records.title: A line on sale reduces that line's total
records.intent: Merchants put individual SKUs on sale. A line with a sale of P percent off has its line total reduced to unitPrice times (1 - P/100) times quantity. The subtotal still reports the pre-sale list total; the sale shows up as that line's lineDiscount and in the order total.
records.given: A cart line has a per-line sale percentage configured for its SKU.
records.then: the line's lineTotal equals unitPrice times (1 - P/100) times quantity, lineDiscount equals the list total minus that lineTotal, subtotal still equals the list total, and total equals the sum of post-sale line totals.

records.title: Order percentage coupon applies after line sales
records.intent: An order-level percentage coupon discounts the cart after per-line sales are applied. The coupon reduces the post-line-discount subtotal (the sum of post-sale line totals), not the original list subtotal.
records.given: A cart already has per-line sales applied and an order percentage coupon of C percent is applied.
records.then: the coupon reduces the running total by C percent of the post-line-discount subtotal, so total equals the post-line-discount subtotal times (1 - C/100), and discountTotal includes both the line sales and the coupon.

records.title: Bulk tier discount stacks with the line sale
records.intent: A SKU can carry a bulk tier: when its line quantity is at or above a minimum, an extra B percent comes off that line. The bulk discount stacks multiplicatively with the line sale, so the net unit price is unitPrice times (1 - sale/100) times (1 - B/100). Below the minimum quantity the bulk tier does not apply.
records.given: A SKU has both a per-line sale and a bulk tier rule with a minimum quantity.
records.when: getQuote is called for a line at or above the minimum quantity, and separately for a line below it.
records.then: at or above the minimum, the line total equals unitPrice times (1 - sale/100) times (1 - B/100) times quantity; below the minimum, only the line sale applies.

records.title: Applying the same coupon code twice counts once
records.intent: Customers sometimes submit the same coupon code more than once. A coupon code that is already applied must not stack with itself, but applying a different valid code must still take effect. canApplyCoupon reports whether a code is not yet applied.
records.given: A cart has an order coupon applied and then the same coupon code is applied a second time.
records.when: getQuote and canApplyCoupon are called, and separately a different valid coupon code is also applied.
records.then: the duplicated code is counted only once so the total matches a single application, canApplyCoupon returns false for an already-applied code and true for a not-yet-applied code, and a distinct second code is not dropped.

records.title: Fixed-amount coupon applies after percentage coupons
records.intent: A fixed-amount coupon (for example five dollars off) is subtracted from the running total only after every percentage coupon has been applied. A fixed coupon never reduces the running total below zero.
records.given: A cart has a percentage coupon and a fixed-amount coupon applied.
records.then: the percentage coupon is applied first to the post-line-discount subtotal, then the fixed amount is subtracted from that result, and the fixed amount subtracted never exceeds the running total.

records.title: Total discount is capped, and the total floors at zero
records.intent: A configured discount cap limits the total discount to at most C percent of the pre-discount subtotal (the list subtotal). The cap applies to the combined line and order discounts. When the combined discount is at or below the cap the cap changes nothing (an exact no-op). The order total can never be negative. Example: on a list subtotal of 20.00 with a 60 percent cap, the maximum discount is 12.00; a combined discount of 16.00 is reduced so the total is 8.00, but a combined discount of 6.50 is left unchanged for a total of 13.50.
records.given: A cart has line and order discounts and a discount cap of C percent of the list subtotal configured.
records.when: getQuote is called when the combined discount exceeds the cap, and separately when it does not.
records.then: when the combined discount exceeds C percent of the list subtotal it is reduced so the discount equals exactly C percent of the list subtotal; when it does not exceed the cap the total is unchanged; and the total is never less than 0.00.

records.title: Tax applies to the post-discount taxable base
records.intent: Tax is charged on the discounted price of taxable goods only. The post-discount total is allocated back to lines in proportion to each line's post-line-discount total; tax-exempt SKUs contribute nothing to the taxable base. Tax equals the configured rate times the taxable base. Example: post-line totals of 18.00 (taxable) and 5.00 (exempt) with a 10 percent order coupon give a post-discount total of 20.70; the taxable line's share is 20.70 times 18/23 = 16.20, so at a 10 percent tax rate the tax is 1.62 and the total is 22.32.
records.given: A cart has taxable and tax-exempt lines, an order coupon, and a tax rate configured.
records.then: the post-discount total is allocated to lines pro rata by post-line-discount line total, only non-exempt lines contribute to the taxable base, taxTotal equals the rate times that base, and total equals the post-discount total plus taxTotal.

records.title: Round once, at the final total
records.intent: Because sales, coupons, and tax can produce fractions of a cent, the final total is rounded half-up to two decimal places. Rounding happens exactly once, on the final total; intermediate line totals, discounts, and tax are kept at full precision and are not rounded per line or per stage. Example: three lines that each compute to 2.004 after a sale plus a 10 percent coupon yield 6.012 times 0.9 = 5.4108, which rounds once to 5.41; rounding each line to 2.00 first would wrongly give 5.40.
records.given: A cart whose discounts produce a total with more than two decimal places.
records.then: total is the full-precision final amount rounded half-up to two decimal places, and no per-line or per-stage rounding is applied before the final total.
